description: Manufacturing Round 2 Run 3: MRP Core Engine Java implementation — Port interfaces, BOM explosion, netting, lot sizing, alternative materials, lead time, adapters, planning engine + REST API

userStories.title: Create MRP Port interfaces and DTOs
acceptanceCriteria: BomLineDto, AlternativeMaterialDto, PlannedOrderDto DTOs exist with all fields
All files compile: cd platform && ./gradlew compileJava succeeds
Package structure is com.auraboot.module.mrp.port and com.auraboot.module.mrp.dto
userStories.notes: These are interfaces only — no implementation yet. The adapters (US-023) will implement them. Use Java 21 features where appropriate. Import BigDecimal, LocalDate, List, Map from java.util and java.math. Check existing module packages (com.auraboot.module.meta, com.auraboot.module.bpm) for conventions.

userStories.title: Implement BomExplosionService with Low Level Code computation
acceptanceCriteria: explodeOneLevel correctly applies quantityPer and lossRate
userStories.notes: TDD required. Write tests first, then implement. Loss rate formula: actualNeed = grossNeed / (1 - lossRate). For lossRate=0 or null, skip adjustment. Use RoundingMode.HALF_UP with scale 4 for precision.

userStories.title: Implement NettingService for net demand calculation
acceptanceCriteria: NettingService.java exists with calculateNetDemand and needsReplenishment methods
Net demand formula is correct: gross - (onHand - allocated) - inTransit + safetyStock
Net demand never returns negative (clamped to 0)
userStories.notes: TDD required. The null-safety for safetyStock is important — some materials may not have safety stock configured.

userStories.title: Implement LotSizing strategies (LFL, FOQ, EOQ)
userStories.description:
Create the lot sizing strategy interface and 3 implementations.

Create:
1. platform/src/main/java/com/auraboot/module/mrp/engine/LotSizingStrategy.java (interface)
```java
public interface LotSizingStrategy {
    String name();
    BigDecimal calculate(BigDecimal netDemand, BigDecimal moq, Map<String, Object> params);
}
```

2. platform/src/main/java/com/auraboot/module/mrp/engine/LotForLotStrategy.java
Returns max(netDemand, moq). Simplest strategy — order exactly what's needed, but at least MOQ.

3. platform/src/main/java/com/auraboot/module/mrp/engine/FixedOrderQtyStrategy.java
Returns ceil(netDemand / fixedQty) * fixedQty, where fixedQty from params. If < MOQ, round up to MOQ.
params key: "fixedOrderQty" (BigDecimal)

4. platform/src/main/java/com/auraboot/module/mrp/engine/EconomicOrderQtyStrategy.java
Classic EOQ = sqrt(2*D*S/H) where D=annual demand, S=order cost, H=holding cost.
params keys: "annualDemand", "orderCost", "holdingCostPerUnit".
Result = max(EOQ, moq).

5. platform/src/main/java/com/auraboot/module/mrp/engine/LotSizingStrategyFactory.java
@Component that holds a Map<String, LotSizingStrategy> and resolves by name.

Test: platform/src/test/java/com/auraboot/module/mrp/engine/LotSizingStrategyTest.java

TDD tests:
- testLotForLot: net=150, moq=0 → 150
- testLotForLotWithMoq: net=50, moq=100 → 100
- testFixedOrderQty: net=250, fixedQty=100, moq=0 → 300
- testFixedOrderQtyWithMoq: net=50, fixedQty=30, moq=100 → 100
- testEoq: D=1200, S=500, H=10 → sqrt(120000)=346.41→347 (ceiling)
- testEoqWithMoq: EOQ=200, moq=500 → 500
- testFactoryResolvesCorrectly
acceptanceCriteria: LotSizingStrategy interface exists with name() and calculate() methods
LotForLotStrategy, FixedOrderQtyStrategy, EconomicOrderQtyStrategy all implement the interface
LotSizingStrategyFactory resolves strategy by name string
All strategies respect MOQ (never return less than MOQ)
userStories.notes: TDD required. MOQ (Minimum Order Quantity) comes from material master data. If moq is null/zero, it means no minimum. For FOQ, fixedQty comes from material config. For EOQ, use Math.ceil for the square root result. All strategies should be @Component for Spring autowiring.

userStories.title: Implement AlternativeMaterialResolver
acceptanceCriteria: ResolvedMaterial.java exists with materialId, isAlternative, conversionFactor
Primary material checked first, then alternatives by priority
Conversion factor applied when checking alternative stock
Returns primary material when all options insufficient
userStories.notes: TDD required. The alternatives should already be sorted by priority from BomQueryPort.getAlternatives(). ConversionFactor means: if factor=1.1, you need 1.1x the quantity of the substitute vs the primary.

userStories.title: Implement LeadTimeService + MrpExceptionReporter
acceptanceCriteria: LeadTimeService.java exists with calculateOrderDate and isLongLeadTime methods
MrpExceptionReporter.java exists with report methods for PAST_DUE, LONG_LEAD_TIME, SHORTAGE
Long lead time threshold is 12 weeks (84 days)
userStories.notes: TDD required. MrpExceptionReporter is stateful per MRP run — create a new instance for each run (prototype scope or manual instantiation). The 12-week threshold is PCBA-specific (IC components often have 12-16 week lead times).

userStories.title: Implement DynamicTable adapters for MRP ports
userStories.description:
Create the DynamicTable adapter implementations that bridge MRP Port interfaces to AuraBoot's mt_* tables.

Create:
1. platform/src/main/java/com/auraboot/module/mrp/adapter/DynamicTableInventoryAdapter.java

Implements InventoryQueryPort. Queries mt_pe_inventory table.
- getOnHandQty: SELECT pe_inv_qty FROM mt_pe_inventory WHERE pe_inv_product_id = ? [AND pe_inv_warehouse_id = ?]
- getInTransitQty: SELECT SUM(pe_po_line_qty) FROM mt_pe_purchase_order_line WHERE status IN ('confirmed') — or return 0 for now
- getAllocatedQty: return 0 for now (will be enhanced later)
- getSafetyStock: SELECT pe_inv_safety_stock FROM mt_pe_inventory WHERE pe_inv_product_id = ?
- getAvailableQty: onHand - allocated

Use JdbcTemplate for dynamic table queries (exception to the MyBatis rule — dynamic table names can't use Mapper). Use TenantContextHolder.getTenantId() for tenant isolation.

2. platform/src/main/java/com/auraboot/module/mrp/adapter/DynamicTableBomAdapter.java

Implements BomQueryPort. Queries mt_pe_bom, mt_pe_bom_line, mt_pe_alternative_material tables.
- computeLowLevelCodes: Load all BOM lines, build graph, compute LLC using BFS.
- getBomLines: SELECT from mt_pe_bom_line WHERE pe_bom_line_bom_id IN (SELECT id FROM mt_pe_bom WHERE pe_bom_product_id = ? AND pe_bom_status = 'enabled')
- hasBom: check if pe_bom exists for materialId
- getLotSizingPolicy: SELECT pe_product_* from mt_pe_product — or return 'lfl' default
- getLeadTime: SELECT from product or return 30 default
- getMoq: SELECT from product or return 1 default
- getAlternatives: SELECT from mt_pe_alternative_material WHERE pe_am_bom_line_id = ? ORDER BY pe_am_priority ASC

Both adapters:
- @Component annotation for Spring autowiring
- @Primary to be the default implementation
- Use @Autowired JdbcTemplate
- Handle null results gracefully (return BigDecimal.ZERO or empty lists)
- Add tenant_id filter manually since JdbcTemplate bypasses TenantLineInterceptor

No unit tests needed for adapters (they depend on real DB). Integration tests in Run 5.
acceptanceCriteria: Both use JdbcTemplate for dynamic table queries
Null-safe: return BigDecimal.ZERO or empty lists for missing data
userStories.notes: These adapters are the bridge between MRP algorithms and AuraBoot's dynamic tables. Dynamic table names use hyphens in URLs but underscores in actual table names: mt_pe_inventory. The JdbcTemplate exception applies here because table names are dynamic. Remember to add tenant_id = TenantContextHolder.getTenantId() to all queries. For fields that don't exist yet on pe_product (lead_time, moq, lot_policy), return sensible defaults.

userStories.title: Implement MrpPlanningEngine orchestrator + REST API
acceptanceCriteria: MrpPlanningEngine.java exists with runMrp() method implementing level-by-level algorithm
Level-by-level processing: processes each LLC level from 0 to max
BOM explosion creates child demands at correct levels
userStories.notes: TDD required. This is the central orchestrator. Mock ALL port interfaces in tests. The controller is a thin layer. For DemandEntry construction from request, use a simple approach for now — just require materialIds + quantities directly, or query sales orders via DynamicTable adapter.

userStories.title: MRP comprehensive unit tests covering edge cases
userStories.description:
Add comprehensive edge-case tests for the MRP engine components.

Add to existing test files or create new test file:
platform/src/test/java/com/auraboot/module/mrp/engine/MrpEdgeCaseTest.java

Tests to write:
1. testEmptyBomReturnsNoChildren: material with no BOM → explodeOneLevel returns empty
2. testCircularBomDetection: A→B→C→A → computeLowLevelCodes should handle without infinite loop (if implemented) or document limitation
3. testZeroDemandSkipped: gross demand = 0 → no planned order generated
4. testNegativeSafetyStockHandled: safety stock = -1 (invalid) → treated as 0
5. testMultipleDemandSourcesSameMaterial: 3 demands for material X → quantities aggregated correctly
6. testAlternativeMaterialWithPartialStock: primary has 50, need 100, alt1 has 60 → returns primary (not sufficient, alt1 also insufficient, so return primary)
7. testLossRateOneHundredPercent: lossRate=1.0 → handle gracefully (division by zero protection)
8. testMoqZero: moq=0 → lot sizing returns exact net demand (no minimum)
9. testAllStrategiesHandleZeroNetDemand: all 3 strategies with netDemand=0 → return 0
10. testMrpRunWithNoSalesOrders: empty demand list → empty result

At least 10 new test methods total. Use mock ports throughout.
acceptanceCriteria: MrpEdgeCaseTest.java (or additions to existing test files) exists
At least 10 new edge case test methods
Tests cover: empty BOM, zero demand, null safety stock, multiple demands, loss rate edge cases
All tests pass: cd platform && ./gradlew test --tests '*mrp*' succeeds
No compilation errors across all MRP module files
userStories.notes: These are additional edge case tests beyond the TDD tests written in previous stories. Focus on boundary conditions and error handling. Don't test the same happy paths already covered — focus on edge cases and error scenarios.
